summary: Complete testing of 2FA login email alerts for new device/IP detection. All features verified working: email notifications created on new IP/device, correct metadata stored, emails NOT sent when IP/device are known.
verified_features: POST /api/enterprise/auth/login-2fa - Detects new IP correctly (is_new_ip: true when IP not in login_history)
POST /api/enterprise/auth/login-2fa - Detects new device correctly (is_new_device: true when User-Agent[:50] not in login_history)
POST /api/enterprise/auth/login-2fa - Creates email_notification with email_type='2fa_login_alert' when new IP or device detected
POST /api/enterprise/auth/login-2fa - Does NOT create email when both IP and device are already known
Email notification metadata contains: employee_name, ip_address, user_agent, timestamp, is_new_ip, is_new_device, login_successful
Email status is 'failed' (expected) due to SendGrid sender not verified - 403 Forbidden
login_history correctly updated with each 2FA login (IP, user_agent, timestamp, method: '2fa')
Background task (BackgroundTasks) used for email sending - doesn't block login response
critical_code_review_comments: Detection logic at lines 222-227 correctly compares current IP/device against login_history
User-Agent comparison uses first 50 characters for consistency (line 224, 227)
Email only sent via background task when is_new_ip OR is_new_device is True (line 261)
SendGrid 403 error is expected - sender email not verified in SendGrid account
Email service correctly saves notification to email_notifications collection regardless of send status
context_for_next_testing_agent: 2FA login email alerts are working. Email notifications with email_type='2fa_login_alert' are created when login is from new IP or device. Email status will be 'failed' because SendGrid sender is not verified (expected behavior). To verify emails are being created, check email_notifications collection in test_database.
notes: SendGrid emails fail with 403 because sender email is not verified. This is expected per requirements - the important verification is that email_notifications are created with correct metadata and that detection logic works correctly.
